insights.title: Timeline Reconstruction
insights.summary: Events must be placed in a consistent sequence. Physical state changes (temperature, rigor, decomposition) anchor the true timeline.
insights.transfer_rule: When testimony contradicts physics, trust physics.

cases.title: The Broken Timeline
cases.summary: A body is found in an apartment. Two witnesses provide alibis, but the physical evidence constrains the timeline in a way that makes one alibi impossible. You must reconstruct the true sequence of events using physics, not testimony.
evidence: The body is found at 08:00 in a locked apartment.
Rigor mortis has set into the jaw and neck but not the limbs.
A half-eaten meal sits on the table — the food is dry and cold.
A digital thermostat log shows the heating was turned off at 23:14 the previous night.
The apartment temperature is 16°C at discovery.
Witness A says they spoke to the victim by phone at 01:00.
Witness B says they saw the victim leaving a shop at 22:30 the previous evening.
A receipt from that shop is timestamped 22:28.
The victim's phone shows no outgoing calls after 23:00.
detective_questions: What does partial rigor mortis tell you about time of death?
How does the thermostat shutoff relate to the victim's last actions?
Which witness statement is contradicted by physical evidence?
What is the earliest and latest possible time of death based on all physical constraints?
Why would a witness lie about the phone call?
analysis_protocol: List physical time anchors separately from testimony.
Build a timeline using ONLY physical evidence first.
Then overlay witness statements and check for conflicts.
Identify the inconsistent testimony and explain why it fails.
scenarios: Witness A is truthful — death occurred after 01:00.
Witness B is truthful — death occurred between 22:30 and 01:00.
Witness A fabricated the phone call — death occurred before 01:00.

evidence_meta.text: The body is found at 08:00 in a locked apartment.
Rigor mortis has set into the jaw and neck but not the limbs.
A half-eaten meal sits on the table — the food is dry and cold.
A digital thermostat log shows the heating was turned off at 23:14 the previous night.
The apartment temperature is 16°C at discovery.
Witness A says they spoke to the victim by phone at 01:00.
Witness B says they saw the victim leaving a shop at 22:30 the previous evening.
A receipt from that shop is timestamped 22:28.
The victim's phone shows no outgoing calls after 23:00.

cases.hidden_truth: Witness A lied about the 01:00 call. The phone records prove no call occurred. Death happened between 23:00 and 00:00, consistent with partial rigor at 08:00 and the thermostat shutoff.
cases.false_narrative: Witness A's phone call at 01:00 proves the victim was alive at that time.

contradictions.description: Witness A claims a 01:00 call, but the phone log shows no calls after 23:00.
Partial rigor at 08:00 suggests death ~8-10 hours prior (22:00-00:00), not after 01:00.
The heating was turned off at 23:14 — likely the victim's last deliberate action.

inference_traps.description: Witness testimony cannot override phone logs and rigor mortis when reconstructing the timeline.
trap_keywords: trust witness a over the phone log

inference_traps.description: Partial rigor at 08:00 is inconsistent with death after 01:00.

required_concept_rules.name: Physical time anchoring
required_concept_rules.description: Uses rigor mortis, temperature, or phone logs to bound the timeline.

required_concept_rules.name: Testimony-physics conflict
required_concept_rules.description: Identifies that a witness statement is contradicted by physical evidence.

required_concept_rules.name: Timeline reconstruction
required_concept_rules.description: Builds a sequence of events from anchored physical facts.

insight_usage_rules.description: Applied narrative fabrication reasoning — the witness constructed a false story.

forbidden_patterns.description: Trusting witness testimony over physical evidence.
